hello, in a previous session we accomplished quite a few things. i have the following session handoff summary for your view to catch up: ============================================================= FLOWGUARD — SESSION HANDOFF SUMMARY Branch: feature/incident-tracking Date: 27 June 2026 ============================================================= This session fixed the remaining issues from the prior session's pending list, plus one new critical bug discovered during testing. Also, the full CRUD features for the dashboard were implemented, specifics as shown: Focus: Centralized management and resolution tracking for security breaches (FR) and spatial anomalies (OD) detected by the AI. Create (C): The Node.js backend automatically creates a new tracked incident report in the PostgreSQL database whenever the AI triggers a critical alert (e.g., an FR tailgating breach or an OD unattended pallet). Additionally, the FM can manually create a new incident record if they spot a hazard on the CCTV feed that requires documentation. Read (R): FM staff utilizes a centralized "Security Command Center" dashboard to fetch and read a sortable, filterable table of all active incidents. The dashboard categorizes incidents by severity, location, and the originating AI source (Facial Recognition vs. Object Detection). Update (U): FM staff can update the status of an incident as it moves through the resolution pipeline (e.g., changing status from "Investigating" to "Escalated to Security" to "Cleared"). They can also append manual resolution notes to the AI's automated log (e.g., "Pallet was successfully relocated to Bay 2 by staff"). Delete (D): FM staff can permanently delete false-positive incidents generated by the AI to keep the analytics accurate. The system also includes a function to archive and remove resolved incidents from the active dashboard at the end of each month for compliance reporting. note that live data seeding (from the AI) has NOT been implemented this session-the data present now is essentially just static data + a bunch of manual logs. ============================================================= OTHER CHANGES MADE THIS SESSION ============================================================= --- CHANGE 1: Delete slide-out animation --- File: client/src/pages/IncidentDashboard.jsx File: client/src/css/IncidentDashboard.css Added `deletingId` state (useState(null)). When a delete is confirmed and the API call succeeds, `setDeletingId(targetId)` is called immediately. The row that is being deleted gets class `inc-row-deleting`, which plays the @keyframes incRowSlideOut animation (opacity 1→0, translateY 0→8px, duration 350ms). After 400ms, the row is actually removed from state with setIncidents(prev => prev.filter(...)) and deletingId is reset to null. CSS added: @keyframes incRowSlideOut { from: opacity 1, translateY 0; to: opacity 0, translateY 8px } .inc-row-deleting { animation: incRowSlideOut 0.35s ease-in forwards; pointer-events: none; } Row class logic in JSX now combines three possible classes: - inc-row-new (slide-in for newly created rows) - inc-row-deleting (slide-out for rows being deleted) - inc-row-manual (warm charcoal tint for manually created rows) --- CHANGE 2: Toast system full overhaul --- File: client/src/pages/IncidentDashboard.jsx File: client/src/css/IncidentDashboard.css BEFORE: Single `toast` string state. One global div (users-toast class) positioned top-right. New toasts overwrote the current toast. No animation for height. Delete success was green. AFTER: - State replaced: `const [toasts, setToasts] = useState([])` (array) - Added: `const toastCounterRef = useRef(0)` for stable unique IDs - New showToast(msg, type = 'success') using useCallback with empty deps: - Increments counter, appends { id, message, type, removing: false } - After 3000ms: sets removing: true on that toast's entry - After 3000ms + 400ms: filters that toast out of array - Each toast has its own independent timer — no overwriting - Three toast types: - 'success' → green tint + green left border (create, update) - 'delete' → red tint + red left border (delete — was wrongly green before) - 'error' → amber tint + amber left border (API failures) - Placement: inc-toast-stack div renders directly between the filter bar and the table container, in document flow (not fixed/absolute). When toasts appear, the table slides down naturally. When they disappear, it slides back up. - CSS animations: @keyframes incToastEnter: max-height 0→80px, opacity 0→1, padding-top/bottom 0→11px, margin-bottom 0→8px (all via forwards fill) @keyframes incToastExit: same in reverse .inc-toast-item { animation: incToastEnter 0.35s ease forwards } .inc-toast-item.removing { animation: incToastExit 0.35s ease forwards } - The max-height + padding animation technique means the toast occupies real document flow height during enter/exit, causing the table to shift down/up smoothly (not jump). - When multiple toasts are stacked and the oldest one collapses, its max-height + margin-bottom animate to 0, naturally pulling toasts below it upward during the animation. - All showToast call sites updated with explicit type: create success → 'success' update success → 'success' delete success → 'delete' all error cases → 'error' --- CHANGE 3: Back-to-top button fix --- File: client/src/pages/IncidentDashboard.jsx ROOT CAUSE: dashboard-layout has `height: 100vh; overflow: hidden`. dashboard-main has `overflow-y: auto`. The body/window NEVER scrolls — window.scrollY is permanently 0. This is why the button never appeared. FIX: - Scroll event listener changed from: window.addEventListener('scroll', handleScroll) → el.scrollTop > 300 check on window.scrollY To: const el = mainRef.current el.addEventListener('scroll', handleScroll) → setShowBackTop(el.scrollTop > 300) - The useEffect captures el = mainRef.current at mount time (safe because the
element is always in the DOM, never conditionally rendered, so the ref is stable). - Button onClick changed from: window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'smooth' }) To: mainRef.current?.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'smooth' }) - Position calculation for horizontal centering was already correct (getBoundingClientRect on mainRef.current → rect.left + rect.width / 2). No change needed there. --- CHANGE 4: Table column truncation --- File: client/src/pages/IncidentDashboard.jsx PROBLEM: Entering an excessively long location or person name caused the table to push rightward, hiding the Actions column (View/Delete buttons) completely, making the table unusable. FIX: Added truncate helper function (module-level, alongside badge helpers): const truncate = (str, n = 30) => str && str.length > n ? str.slice(0, n) + '…' : str; Applied in table rows only: - Location cell: {truncate(incident.camera_location)} with title={incident.camera_location} for hover tooltip - Person name cell: {truncate(incident.person_name)} with title={incident.person_name} on the parent div for hover tooltip The detail modal still renders selectedIncident.camera_location and selectedIncident.person_name in full — no truncation there. --- CHANGE 5: Modal text overflow fix --- File: client/src/css/IncidentDashboard.css PROBLEM: Long text values in the detail modal (especially the Location field when entering a stress-test string like many 'e' chars) would overflow past the modal's right edge horizontally, stretching the modal and making it ugly/unusable. FIX: - Added to .inc-detail-value: overflow-wrap: break-word; word-break: break-word; min-width: 0; - Added to .inc-detail-item: min-width: 0; The min-width: 0 on .inc-detail-item is critical — CSS grid children have min-width: auto by default, which allows them to overflow the grid column boundary. Setting min-width: 0 allows the grid cell to shrink to its assigned column width. The word-break then wraps the text within that constrained width. ============================================================= CURRENT STATE OF KEY FILES ============================================================= server/models/IncidentLog.js - 4 extra columns: severity, source, resolutionStatus, notes - paranoid: true (soft delete) - All nullable/defaulted so AI scan-frame route still works server/routes/incident.js - POST /scan-frame — untouched AI route - POST / — FM manual creation, verifyToken - GET / — no auth (returns all, ordered DESC by createdAt) - GET /:id — no auth - PATCH /:id — verifyToken, validates resolutionStatus against allowlist - DELETE /:id — verifyToken client/src/pages/IncidentDashboard.jsx - Full CRUD wired to API - One-time seed of 12 static incidents gated by localStorage flag 'flowguard_incidents_seeded' (set on first successful seed) - Filters: search, severity, source, resolution status (all client-side on the fetched data) - Stacked toast system (type-aware, 3s timers, non-overwriting) - Row animations: slide-in (new), slide-out (deleting), manual tint - Back-to-top button (scroll listener on mainRef.current) - Table truncation at 30 chars for location + person name - Detail modal: full text, word-wrap, 2-col grid - Edit mode inside detail modal (resolutionStatus + notes) - Create modal (source locked to Manual) - Delete confirmation modal (uses existing Management.css classes) client/src/css/IncidentDashboard.css - All badge styles: severity, source (inc. manual), resolution status - Stat cards, filter bar, search input, selects - Detail modal, create modal, form grid, textarea, save button - Loading bar - Manual row tint (.management-table .inc-row-manual) - Row slide-in: @keyframes incRowSlideIn + .inc-row-new - Row slide-out: @keyframes incRowSlideOut + .inc-row-deleting - Toast stack: @keyframes incToastEnter/incToastExit + type variants - Back-to-top: .inc-back-top + .visible + hover client/src/App.jsx — /incidents route with ACCESS.FM_ONLY (done prev session) client/src/components/Sidebar.jsx — Incident Dashboard NavLink in isFM block (done prev session) ============================================================= KNOWN GOOD / NO OUTSTANDING ISSUES ============================================================= - All 5 CRUD operations work and persist across refresh - Seed flag prevents re-seeding on refresh - Table layout is stable regardless of input length - Modal handles long text gracefully (wraps, doesn't overflow) - Back-to-top appears/hides correctly when scrolling dashboard-main - Toast stack: multiple concurrent toasts, each with own 3s timer, correct colours (green=success, red=delete, amber=error) - Delete and create both have smooth row animations ============================================================= WHAT HAS NOT BEEN IMPLEMENTED (OUT OF SCOPE THIS SESSION) ============================================================= - Live data seeding from AI scan-frame into the dashboard (deferred) - No changes to any other page, route, or component ============================================================= of course, further testing may reveal previously unseen issues. ---- apart from the session handoff, you may also analyse the current codebase to catch up with what is already present, before we work on the item for this session.

BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

---
name: project-overview
description: FlowGuard project stack, roles, and overall architecture
metadata:
  type: project
---

FlowGuard is a security management system for Harrison Food Factory. Three roles: FM (Facility Manager), Staff (Security Staff), Tenant.

**Stack**: React (Vite) frontend + Node.js/Express backend + PostgreSQL via Sequelize. Frontend on `client/`, backend on `server/`.

**Why:** Assignment project, branch `feature/incident-tracking` is the active feature branch. Main branch is `main`.

**Auth**: JWT tokens stored in localStorage (`accessToken`, `userName`, `userRole`). `verifyToken` middleware on protected routes. Frontend uses `ProtectedRoute` with `allowedRoles` prop.

**Roles + access constants** defined in `client/src/constants/roles.js` — ACCESS.FM_ONLY, ACCESS.FM_STAFF, ACCESS.FM_TENANT.

**Sidebar** (`client/src/components/Sidebar.jsx`) has conditional NavLinks per role. isFM, isStaff, isTenant flags.

**Key pages** (FM-only): User Management `/users`, Security Review `/security-review`, Incident Dashboard `/incidents`, Tenant Onboarding `/tenant-management`, Settings `/settings`.

**CSS pattern**: Each page has its own CSS file. Shared base: `Dashboard.css` (layout), `Management.css` (table, modals), `Users.css` (action buttons). Page-specific file extends these patterns.

**How to apply:** Maintain this pattern when adding new pages — don't create monolithic CSS, extend the shared base.

content.text:
Perfect! I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive analysis:

## FlowGuard Live Data Flow Analysis

Based on my exploration of the codebase, here's the current pattern for how live data flows from the AI backend to the React frontend:

### **CURRENT PATTERN: Polling with `setInterval` + Axios**

FlowGuard uses **timer-based polling** across all real-time features. There is **NO WebSocket or SSE** currently implemented.

---

## 1. **ObjectDetection.jsx** — Polling Pattern
**File:** `client/src/pages/ObjectDetection.jsx`

**Live Data Flows:**
- **People Count**: Polled every **5 seconds** (reduced from 2s to reduce noise when AI offline)
  ```javascript
  const peopleInterval = setInterval(fetchPeopleCount, 5000);
  ```
  - Endpoint: `GET /ai/api/yolo/people-count`
  - Returns: `{ count, detection_active }`

- **Detection Alerts**: Polled every **15 seconds**
  ```javascript
  const alertsInterval = setInterval(fetchAlerts, 15000);
  ```
  - Endpoint: `GET /api/detection-alerts`
  - Returns: Array of alert objects with status, zone, duration

- **Local Frame Analysis**: Every **1400ms** (in-browser camera → local canvas → AI)
  ```javascript
  frameInterval = setInterval(analyzeCurrentFrame, 1400);
  ```
  - Endpoint: `POST /ai/api/yolo/analyze-frame` (sends base64 JPEG)
  - Returns: `{ detections: [...], count, detection_active, camera_status }`

**Key Implementation Details:**
- Uses `axios` with 3-6 second timeouts
- Uses `useCallback` for stable function references
- Cleans up intervals on unmount
- Handles AI offline state gracefully

---

## 2. **Cameras.jsx** — Static Data (No Real-Time)
**File:** `client/src/pages/Cameras.jsx`

**Finding:** This is a **static demo** page. No live updates. Just displays hardcoded camera feed metadata.

---

## 3. **GateScanner.jsx** — Polling Pattern
**File:** `client/src/pages/GateScanner.jsx`

**Live Data Flow:**
- **Perimeter Scan**: Every **1200ms**
  ```javascript
  const scanInterval = setInterval(() => performPerimeterScan(), 1200);
  ```
  - Endpoint: `POST /ai/user/recognize` (sends canvas base64 image)
  - Returns: `{ box, liveness_ratio, user: { name } }`
  - Then posts recognized face to: `POST /api/attendance/scan`

**State Machine:** Uses staged scanning states (SYSTEM_ACTIVE → PRESENCE_DETECTED → TARGET_LOCKING → LIVENESS_CHECK → SECURE_MATCH/UNKNOWN_QUERY)

---

## 4. **VPatrol.jsx** — Polling Pattern
**File:** `client/src/pages/VPatrol.jsx`

**Live Data Flow:**
- **Live Scan**: Every **1200ms** (same as GateScanner)
  ```javascript
  const scanInterval = setInterval(() => { performLiveScan(); }, 1200);
  ```
  - Endpoint: `POST /ai/user/recognize`
  - Returns face box, liveness ratio, user identity

- **Incident Logs**: Fetched once on mount, then new logs created via POST
  ```javascript
  axios.get(NODE_SERVER_URL, { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` } })
  ```
  - Endpoint: `GET /api/security/logs`
  - Returns array of security events

---

## 5. **Server Routes — No SSE/WebSocket**

### `server/routes/detectionAlerts.js`
- `GET /api/detection-alerts` — Returns all alerts (with optional status filter)
- `POST /api/detection-alerts` — Create alert
- `PUT /api/detection-alerts/:id` — Update alert status
- **Pattern**: Simple REST, no streaming

### `server/routes/security.js`
- `POST /api/security/logs` — AI or VPatrol posts security events here
- `GET /api/security/logs` — FM reviews with optional status filter
- `PATCH /api/security/logs/:id/review` — FM marks as reviewed/false positive/escalated
- **Pattern**: Request-response, no real-time push

### `server/routes/incident.js`
- `POST /api/incident/scan-frame` — AI submits frame for analysis
- `GET /api/incident` — Fetch all incidents (with optional search)
- `PATCH /api/incident/:id` — Update resolution status
- **Pattern**: REST CRUD, no streaming

---

## **IncidentDashboard.jsx** — Current Pattern
**File:** `client/src/pages/IncidentDashboard.jsx`

**Live Data Flow:**
- **One-time fetch on mount** with seed logic
  ```javascript
  const fetchIncidents = useCallback(async () => {
    const res = await axios.get('/api/incident', { headers });
    setIncidents(Array.isArray(res.data) ? res.data : []);
  }, [showToast]);
  ```
- **NO polling interval** — only fetches once
- **Manual updates** via CRUD buttons (Create, Edit, Delete)

**Current Limitation:** If an AI detection creates an incident on the server, the dashboard **won't see it** until the user manually refreshes or clicks "fetch again".

---

## **PATTERN TO REUSE FOR INCIDENT DASHBOARD**

Based on the codebase, here's what you should replicate:

### **Option A: Quick Polling (Recommended for your case)**
```javascript
useEffect(() => {
  // Fetch incidents once on mount
  fetchIncidents();
  
  // Poll for new incidents every 10-15 seconds
  const incidentInterval = setInterval(fetchIncidents, 10000);
  
  return () => clearInterval(incidentInterval);
}, []);
```

**Endpoint to use:**
```
GET /api/incident?limit=50
```

**Why this works:**
- Matches existing ObjectDetection polling pattern (5s, 15s)
- No backend changes needed (endpoint already exists)
- Simple to implement
- Works across all network conditions

### **Option B: If you want real-time push (Future Enhancement)**
You'd need to add WebSocket or SSE to the server (currently NOT implemented):

```javascript
// SSE approach (simpler than WebSocket)
const eventSource = new EventSource('/api/incident/stream');
eventSource.addEventListener('new-incident', (e) => {
  const incident = JSON.parse(e.data);
  setIncidents(prev => [incident, ...prev]);
});
```

But this requires backend changes to `/server/index.js`.

---

## **Summary of Data Flow Architecture**

| Component | Data Source | Interval | Endpoint | Transport |
|-----------|-------------|----------|----------|-----------|
| ObjectDetection | AI YOLO | 5s (people), 15s (alerts), 1.4s (frames) | `/ai/api/yolo/people-count`, `/api/detection-alerts` | Polling |
| GateScanner | AI Face Rec | 1.2s | `/ai/user/recognize` | Polling |
| VPatrol | AI + DB | 1.2s (scan), 1x fetch (logs) | `/ai/user/recognize`, `/api/security/logs` | Polling + manual POST |
| IncidentDashboard | DB only | **1x on mount** | `/api/incident` | One-time fetch |

**Key Insight:** All live features use short polling intervals (1.2–15 seconds). The IncidentDashboard currently lacks polling—it's static until user interaction.

---

## **Recommended Implementation for Incident Dashboard**

To match the existing pattern and enable live updates:

1. **Add polling interval** to IncidentDashboard (10-15s seems reasonable)
2. **Optionally filter** by `resolutionStatus=Active` to show only open incidents
3. **Optionally add** manual refresh button (already in code)
4. **Keep** the manual create/edit/delete actions as-is

This uses the **exact same polling pattern** ObjectDetection uses, so you're reusing a proven approach.

input.prompt:
I'm building a live data integration for an incident security dashboard in FlowGuard. I need a detailed implementation plan. Here's the full context:

## Project Stack
- React (Vite) frontend at `client/src/`
- Node.js/Express backend at `server/`
- PostgreSQL via Sequelize
- Python AI service (FastAPI) at `ai-service/` running on port 8501

## What the AI Service Does
The AI service (`ai-service/main.py`) has a background YOLO detection loop. When it detects an unattended object, it fires an HTTP POST to `{NODE_SERVER_URL}/api/detection-alerts` with this payload:
```json
{
  "zone_name": "Zone A",
  "camera_location": "Webcam Feed",
  "status": "Active",
  "object_class": "backpack",
  "duration_seconds": 300,
  "person_name": "John Doe"
}
```

This currently saves to the `DetectionAlert` model but does NOT create an `IncidentLog` entry — so it never shows up in the incident dashboard.

## Key Files

### `server/routes/detectionAlerts.js` (current POST handler)
```javascript
router.post('/', async (req, res) => {
  // saves to DetectionAlert model
  // does NOT touch IncidentLog
});
```

### `server/models/IncidentLog.js` (fields)
- camera_location, status (e.g. UNAUTHORIZED_ACCESS/UNATTENDED_OBJECT/etc), person_name, confidence_score, severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low), source (Facial Recognition/Object Detection/Manual), resolutionStatus (default: Active), notes

### `client/src/pages/IncidentDashboard.jsx` (relevant state/logic)
- `const [incidents, setIncidents] = useState([])`
- `const [newRowId, setNewRowId] = useState(null)` — tracks single new row for slide-in animation
- Row class: `incident.id === newRowId ? 'inc-row-new' : ''`
- Static seed: On mount, `STATIC_INCIDENTS` array is seeded if localStorage flag `flowguard_incidents_seeded` is not set. This needs to be REMOVED.
- Current fetch: one-time `fetchIncidents()` on mount via axios GET /api/incident
- NO polling currently — incidents only update on user CRUD actions

### Existing polling pattern (from `client/src/pages/ObjectDetection.jsx`)
- `setInterval(fetchAlerts, 15000)` — polls every 15 seconds
- Clean up with `return () => clearInterval(id)`
- Uses `useCallback` with stable deps

## Requirements
1. **Server bridge**: When AI posts a detection alert to `/api/detection-alerts`, ALSO create an `IncidentLog` entry so it appears in the dashboard. Map fields appropriately (severity from duration_seconds, status from object type, source = "Object Detection").
2. **Remove static seed**: Remove all seed code from `IncidentDashboard.jsx` — the STATIC_INCIDENTS array, T() helper, SEED_FLAG constant, and the seedAndFetch logic. Replace with a simple `fetchIncidents()` on mount.
3. **Add polling**: Poll `GET /api/incident` every 10 seconds (background, no loading bar). Detect new incidents (IDs not in current state) and animate them with `inc-row-new` class.
4. **Handle multiple new rows**: The current `newRowId` (single value) is insufficient for polling — multiple rows may arrive simultaneously. Change to `newRowIds` (a Set). Update the row class logic and the manual create handler accordingly.
5. **AI vs Manual counter**: Add a small styled counter showing total AI-detected incidents vs manually logged incidents. Computed from the full `incidents` array. Positioned between the 4 stat cards and the filter bar.

## Design Constraints
- The source counter should be SMALL — pill/chip style, not another large stat card. "AI Detected: N" | "Manually Logged: N"
- Polling must be SILENT — no loading spinner/bar flicker on background poll
- New rows from polling get `inc-row-new` animation just like manually created rows
- Do NOT modify any other pages (ObjectDetection, GateScanner, VPatrol, etc.)
- The CSS must match the site's dark theme: `background: #1e293b`, `border: 1px solid #334155`, slate/blue palette

## Question to address in the plan
1. How should `pollForNewIncidents` access current state without stale closures? (Hint: use the functional form of `setIncidents(prev => ...)`)
2. What's the exact field mapping from DetectionAlert POST payload to IncidentLog?
3. Should the AI vs Manual counter use `incidents` (full list) or `filtered` (currently filtered)? (Use `incidents` — total counts for an "at a glance" summary)

Please provide a detailed step-by-step implementation plan including: exact code snippets where helpful, field mapping table, stale closure solution, and CSS approach for the counter chips.

Everything is correctly placed. All five steps are complete. Here's a summary of every change made:

---

**`server/routes/detectionAlerts.js`**
- Added `IncidentLog` to the model require
- Added `severityFromDuration(seconds)` helper (Low/Medium/High/Critical based on duration)
- After the AI's detection alert is saved and the `201` is sent, a fire-and-forget `IncidentLog.create()` runs — the AI never waits for it and will always get its `201` back regardless

**`client/src/pages/IncidentDashboard.jsx`**
- Removed the `T()` helper, entire `STATIC_INCIDENTS` array (127 lines), `SEED_FLAG` constant, and the `seedAndFetch` async loop — gone entirely
- `fetchIncidents` now accepts a `silent` flag — when `true`, no loading bar and no error toast (used by the poll)
- `newRowId` (single null) → `newRowIds` (Set), `handleCreate` and the row class logic updated accordingly — multiple rows can animate simultaneously
- `pollForNewIncidents` callback: fetches every 10s silently, uses `setIncidents(prev => ...)` functional updater to diff against current state without stale closures, adds newly seen IDs to `newRowIds` for the slide-in animation then removes them after 700ms
- `sourceCounts` useMemo: computed from full `incidents` array (not filtered), counts AI (non-Manual source) vs Manual
- Source counter chips JSX rendered between the stat cards and the loading bar

**`client/src/css/IncidentDashboard.css`**
- Added `.inc-source-counter`, `.inc-source-chip`, `.inc-source-chip-ai` (purple, matching existing OD badge), `.inc-source-chip-manual` (slate, matching existing manual badge), `.inc-source-chip-divider`
